Scenarios.title: Load first-line osimertinib evidence — clean upload, curator approves, persisted
Scenarios.description: Upload and ingest five OA research articles via the ingestion console. Ingestion translation de-duplicates and normalizes content; metadata linking extracts entities and trial-to-regulatory links; the Knowledge Curator approves persistence to the Vector DB.

Scenarios.title: Load with a messy pool — license and PHI guardrails, routed to the curator
Scenarios.description: Upload a mixed research package including articles and GEO dataset candidates. Ingestion flags exclusions and routes the batch to the Knowledge Curator for human review before anything is persisted.

study.title: Human review needed — mixed article and dataset pool

Scenarios.title: Load synthetic ELN/LIMS records — curator approves with required labeling, persisted
Scenarios.description: Upload synthetic ELN and LIMS lab-operation records derived from public GEO structure. Metadata linking associates samples to GEO series; the curator approves with required synthetic provenance labeling.

Scenarios.title: Load a patient-derived candidate — blocked by the curator, never persisted
Scenarios.description: Upload a package containing a patient-derived GEO dataset candidate (GSE301973). The Knowledge Curator denies persistence; nothing is written to the Vector DB.

Scenarios.title: Query an empty knowledge base — no grounded answer (demo step 1)
Scenarios.description: Run the headline demo query against an empty Vector DB. Search & Chat should report that no grounded answer is available. Run before ING-001 to show the before/after narrative.
Scenarios.sampleQuestion: What are the known mechanisms of resistance to osimertinib in EGFR-mutated NSCLC, and which clinical-trial and regulatory evidence support its first-line use?

Scenarios.title: Same query after ingestion — grounded answer with citations (demo step 3)
Scenarios.description: Run the same osimertinib resistance query after ING-001 completes. Search & Chat should return a grounded answer with citations from persisted knowledge.
Scenarios.sampleQuestion: What are the known mechanisms of resistance to osimertinib in EGFR-mutated NSCLC, and which clinical-trial and regulatory evidence support its first-line use?

Scenarios.title: Load an empty and truncated batch — insufficient data, nothing persisted
Scenarios.description: Upload a batch containing an empty file and a truncated protocol fragment. Ingestion translation should return Insufficient Data; nothing is persisted.

Scenarios.title: Messy pool reviewed by curator — exclusions accepted, admitted content persisted
Scenarios.description: Same mixed article and GEO pool as ING-002, but the Knowledge Curator approves admitted content after exclusion review. Articles and accepted GEO datasets are persisted.

Scenarios.title: EU-scoped query — grounded answer missing regional policy context
Scenarios.description: Query the EU/EMA approved first-line indication after ING-001. Search & Chat should answer with trial/label citations; Curate should flag missing EU regional policy context (HLS-REGION-EU-400).
Scenarios.sampleQuestion: What is the EU/EMA approved first-line indication for osimertinib in EGFR-mutated NSCLC?

Scenarios.title: Ambiguous resistance query — clarification needed before retrieval
Scenarios.description: Run a broad osimertinib resistance query without study, region, or time scope. Search & Chat should request clarification before grounding an answer.
Scenarios.sampleQuestion: What does the evidence say about osimertinib resistance?

Scenarios.title: Multi-turn grounded chat — Curate reviews accumulated responses
Scenarios.description: Run two grounded queries in the same session (resistance mechanisms, then FLAURA context) after ING-001, then Curate. Curation should review the full chatResponses set.
Scenarios.sampleQuestion: What are the known mechanisms of resistance to osimertinib in EGFR-mutated NSCLC?
